moves.note: Monitor strong chip, no immediate buy

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the single name mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETFs and de-risk the portfolio.
Increase QQQI to 25% for greater income exposure and portfolio stability.
Buy XLV at 8% to diversify into the high-quality healthcare sector.
Maintain a significant cash buffer for capital preservation and future opportunities.
Monitor ASML for potential entry, but do not add now.
conservative.read: This conservative approach targets around 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdown. Expected swings will be smaller, but growth potential is also limited.

moves.note: Increase exposure to strong AI name

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate and manage overall risk.
Sell SOXL entirely due to it being below its stop and to reduce overall leverage.
Trim SPAL to 3% to maintain a small, managed exposure to leveraged ETFs.
Buy LRCX at 10% and TXN at 8% to add high-scoring, quality chip exposure.
Increase CRWD to 15% to lean into a strong performing AI/Software name.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer to balance growth with risk management.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets around 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while adding high-quality tech names. This mode aims for a blend of growth and stability, with moderate drawdown potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to adhere to the single name concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely as it is below its stop, maintaining strict stop discipline.
Trim SMH to 8% to reduce exposure to a downtrending semiconductor index.
Buy LRCX at 15% and TXN at 10% to aggressively lean into the highest-scoring chip setups.
Increase CRWD to 20% to press a strong performing AI/Software name with an uptrend.
Maintain a smaller cash buffer, focusing on deploying capital into high-conviction trades.
aggressive.read: This aggressive approach targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretch growth. It concentrates in high-scoring setups and uses leverage where appropriate, but strictly adheres to stop-loss discipline. This mode carries significantly higher drawdown risk, and leveraged ETFs can experience daily decay, leading to potentially rapid losses if not managed with strict stops.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and align with the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETFs and high-beta exposure.
Trim CRWD to 5% and SMH to 5% to reduce high-beta single name and chip sector exposure.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize exposure to income-generating ETFs for monthly distributions.
Maintain a significant cash buffer to prioritize low drawdown and reliable income.
Focus on stable, income-producing assets over growth-oriented holdings.
income.read: This income approach targets around 1% per month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions. It significantly de-risks the portfolio by eliminating leveraged ETFs and high-beta single names. While aiming for low drawdown and reliable income, distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff, meaning it's not a savings account.
